subject: Mathematics

unit.unitOverview: Become confident with every number from 0 to 100. Learn how tens and ones give a number its value, then use that understanding to count, compare, order and continue number sequences.
learningPath: Preview the goals and key idea

provenance.transformation: Selected and structured from standardized workbook rows; wording retained or shortened for screen presentation.

outcomes: Read, write and represent numbers from 0 to 100.
Identify the tens and ones in a two-digit number.
Count forwards and backwards in ones, twos and tens.
Estimate a collection and count to check.
Order numbers from smallest to greatest.
Use ordinal numbers such as 1st, 2nd and 3rd.

concepts.title: The 100 square
concepts.explanation: A 100 square is a number map. Across a row the number increases by 1; down a column it increases by 10.

concepts.title: Tens and ones
concepts.explanation: In a two-digit number, the left digit tells the tens and the right digit tells the ones.

concepts.title: Represent a number
concepts.explanation: Show the same number with a numeral, words, tens and ones, or bundles and single objects.

concepts.title: Count collections
concepts.explanation: Estimate first. Make groups of ten, count the tens, then add any leftover ones.

concepts.title: Compare and order
concepts.explanation: Compare the tens first. If the tens match, compare the ones. The open side of > or < faces the greater number.
concepts.example: 75 > 57 because 7 tens is greater than 5 tens

concepts.title: Sequences and position
concepts.explanation: Find how each number changes, check the rule, and repeat it. Ordinal numbers describe position in a line.

explorations.title: Dates at the market
explorations.context: Halima groups 47 dates into bundles of ten so she can count quickly.
explorations.prompt: If she has 6 bundles and 3 loose dates, how many dates does she have?
explorations.hint: Six bundles are 6 tens. Write the tens digit before the 3 ones.

explorations.title: Find the hidden house
explorations.context: A house number is one step after 42 and one step before 44 on a street map.
explorations.prompt: What is the hidden house number?

explorations.title: Pack reading cards
explorations.context: Yusuf packs 35 reading cards as complete packs of ten and loose cards.
explorations.prompt: How many complete packs and loose cards make 35?
explorations.hint: The left digit gives the packs of ten.

explorations.title: Estimate the bottle collection
explorations.context: Noor sees about five groups of ten bottles and two loose bottles.
explorations.prompt: What exact count should she expect after grouping?

explorations.title: Choose the bus
explorations.context: One bus can carry 64 passengers and another can carry 46.
explorations.explanation: 64 is greater because 6 tens is more than 4 tens.

explorations.title: Water-container pattern
explorations.context: A family records 12, 22, 32 and 42 litres on four days.
explorations.prompt: If the pattern continues, what comes next?
explorations.hint: Find how much is added each day.

visualModels.title: Base-ten blocks
visualModels.purpose: Build a number with tens rods and ones cubes.

visualModels.title: Number line
visualModels.purpose: Locate, count and compare numbers between 0 and 100.

visualModels.title: Ten frames
visualModels.purpose: See complete groups of ten and leftover ones.

visualModels.title: 100 square
visualModels.purpose: See one-more and ten-more patterns across rows and columns.

visualModels.title: Counters and groups
visualModels.purpose: Estimate and regroup loose objects into tens.

visualModels.title: Ordinal line
visualModels.purpose: Show how positions change when people join or leave a line.

methods.title: Read a two-digit number
steps: Say four tens and seven ones.

workedExamples.title: Fill gaps in a row
workedExamples.solution: The rule is add 1. The missing numbers are 23 and 25.

workedExamples.title: Find a digit's value
workedExamples.prompt: In 85, what is the value of 8?
workedExamples.solution: The 8 is in the tens place, so it is worth 80. Therefore 85 = 80 + 5.

workedExamples.title: Count a collection
workedExamples.prompt: Sagal makes 4 rings of ten mangoes and has 6 left. How many mangoes?

workedExamples.title: Compare two numbers
workedExamples.solution: Compare tens: 7 tens is greater than 5 tens, so 75 > 57.

workedExamples.title: Continue a sequence
workedExamples.prompt: Start at 37 and add ten until 77.

workedExamples.title: Change an ordinal position
workedExamples.prompt: Abdi is 4th. Two people at the front leave. What position is Abdi now?
workedExamples.solution: Abdi moves forward two places, so he is now 2nd.

workedExamples.title: Make the smallest odd number
workedExamples.prompt: Use 3, 5, 7 and 9 to make the smallest odd two-digit number.
workedExamples.solution: 35: put the smallest digit in the tens place, then the smallest remaining odd digit in the ones place.

workedExamples.title: Represent one number four ways
workedExamples.prompt: Show 62 as a numeral, words, tens and ones, and expanded form.

workedExamples.title: Compare equal tens
workedExamples.solution: The tens are equal, so compare ones. 3 < 8, therefore 63 < 68.

workedExamples.title: Estimate and check
workedExamples.prompt: A pile looks close to 50. Five groups of ten and 4 loose objects are counted. What is the exact total?
workedExamples.solution: 5 tens are 50; 50 + 4 = 54. The estimate of about 50 was sensible.

practice.prompt: How many tens and ones are in 53?
practice.hint: Read the left digit, then the right digit.
Start with the number with no tens, then compare tens and ones.

practice.prompt: Using 2, 4, 6 and 8, make the largest possible even two-digit number.
practice.hint: Use the largest digit in the tens place.

practice.prompt: Waris is 2nd in line. Three people join behind her. What position is she in now?
practice.hint: People behind Waris do not change her place.

practice.prompt: Noor has 7 rings of ten bottles and 2 extra. How many bottles?
Ibrahim is the 9th child. How many children came before him?

activities.title: Make Your Own 100 Square
steps: Write 1 to 100, ten numbers per row.
Colour multiples of ten and then even numbers.

activities.title: Bundles of Ten
activities.materials: 47 sticks, stones or beans; string or bands
steps: Count full bundles and leftover ones.
Build other numbers called by a partner.

activities.title: The Crocodile Compare Game
steps: Draw > or < with the open side facing the greater number.

activities.title: Market Counting Investigation
activities.materials: Dates, stones or other countable objects
steps: Compare the estimate with the exact count.

activities.title: Ordinal Line-Up
steps: Line up five or more people.
Call an ordinal and an action.

activities.title: Number Sequence Snake
steps: Draw a long snake divided into boxes.
Choose a start and a counting rule.
Leave gaps for a partner to solve.

rules.title: Place Value Rule
rules.text: Left digit = tens. Right digit = ones. 47 = 4 tens + 7 ones = 40 + 7.

rules.title: Comparing Rule
rules.text: Compare tens first. If the tens are equal, compare ones. The open side of > or < faces the bigger number.

rules.title: Odd and Even Rule
rules.text: Even numbers end in 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8. Odd numbers end in 1, 3, 5, 7 or 9.

terms: What a digit is worth because of its position
The two places in a two-digit number
Even ends in 0,2,4,6,8; odd ends in 1,3,5,7,9
commonMistakes: Say the tens first: five tens and one one makes 51.
The 4 is in the tens place, so it means 40.
The open side faces the bigger number.

fluency.hint: Write the tens digit before the ones digit.
fluency.errorFeedback: Four tens are 40, then add 3 ones.
Seven tens and six ones make 76.
Adding ten changes 3 tens to 4 tens.
Subtracting ten changes 8 tens to 7 tens.
Seven tens is greater than six tens.
The ones stay 2 while the tens increase.
Five tens is less than nine tens.
Nine tens and four ones make 94.

realProblems.prompt: Sumaya prepares 3 trays of 10 sambusas and 5 loose sambusas. How many are there?
Noor has 7 rings of ten bottles and 2 extra bottles. How many bottles?
One minibus has 64 seats and another has 46. Which capacity is greater?
realProblems.errorFeedback: Six tens is greater than four tens.

realProblems.prompt: Order these prices from cheapest to most expensive: 25, 52, 35, 53, 33.
realProblems.errorFeedback: Start with the two-tens number, then the three-tens numbers.

realProblems.prompt: A bus is at kilometre 75 and travels back 10 kilometres. At which kilometre is it now?
realProblems.hint: Count back one group of ten.

realProblems.prompt: Ibrahim is the 9th child in line. How many children are before him?

reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: Compare tens first. Seven tens is greater than five tens, so 75 is greater than 57.

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Explain why the 8 in 85 is worth 80, not 8.
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: The 8 is in the tens place, so it represents 8 tens, which equals 80.
Add 10 each time. The ones digit stays 7 and the tens digit increases by one.

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Why is grouping 46 objects into tens safer than counting every object one by one?
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: Groups of ten are easier to count and check: four groups and six ones make 46.

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Explain how you know 63 comes before 68 when ordering numbers.
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: Both numbers have 6 tens, so compare the ones. Three ones is less than eight ones.

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Waris is 2nd and three people join behind her. Explain why her position does not change.
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: The new people joined behind Waris, so nobody moved in front of her. She remains 2nd.

questions.question: What number has 4 tens and 7 ones?
questions.explanation: 4 tens are 40; add 7 ones to make 47.

questions.question: What is the value of 8 in 85?
questions.explanation: The 8 is in the tens place.

questions.question: Which comparison is correct?
questions.explanation: Six tens is greater than three tens.

questions.question: A number line jumps +10 from 37 three times. Where does it land?
Five complete ten frames and two filled spaces represent…
questions.explanation: Five tens and two ones make 52.

questions.question: On a 100 square, what number is directly below 46?
What is 10 less than 82?
questions.hint: Decrease the tens digit by one.

questions.question: Noor has 7 rings of ten bottles and 2 extra. How many bottles?
questions.hint: Combine seven tens and two ones.

questions.question: Ibrahim is 9th in line. How many children are before him?
Why is 75 greater than 57?
questions.explanation: Compare tens before ones: seven tens is greater than five tens.

questions.question: Which statement best explains 42, 52, 62, 72?
questions.hint: Notice which digit stays the same.
questions.explanation: The sequence adds ten; the ones digit remains 2.

selfAssessment: I can read, write and picture any number from 0 to 100.
I can say how many tens and ones are in a two-digit number.
I can count on and back in ones, twos and tens.
I can estimate and count to check.
I can compare and order numbers.
I can continue a number sequence.
